exercises.title: Positive or Negative
exercises.instructions:
Write a program that checks if the number 7 is positive or negative.
Print "Positive" if it is greater than 0, otherwise print "Negative".

exercises.title: If: Positive Number
exercises.instructions: Check if the number is greater than 0 and print 'Positive' when it is.
exercises.hints: Use the > operator to compare num with 0

exercises.title: If: Is Even
exercises.instructions: If the number is divisible by 2, print 'Even'.
exercises.hints: A number is even when num % 2 equals 0

exercises.title: Even or Odd
exercises.instructions:
Given number = 17, check if it is even or odd.
Print "Even" if divisible by 2, otherwise print "Odd".
exercises.hints: Use the modulo operator % to check divisibility.

exercises.title: If: Big Enough
exercises.instructions: If the age is 18 or more, print 'Adult'.

exercises.title: If: Passing Mark
exercises.instructions: If the score is 50 or above, print 'Pass'.
exercises.hints: Use >= so a score of exactly 50 still passes

exercises.title: If: Empty Array
exercises.instructions: If the array has 0 items, print 'Empty'.
exercises.hints: items.length returns the number of items in the array

exercises.title: If: Member Check
exercises.instructions: If the letter 'a' is in the word 'banana', print 'Found'.
exercises.hints: Use .includes() to check if the letter is in the word

exercises.title: If: Boolean Flag
exercises.instructions: If isRaining is true, print 'Take an umbrella'.
exercises.hints: A boolean variable can be used directly as the condition
